# **भारतीय बीमा विवियामक और विकास प्राविकरण (बीमाकतााओं का पंजीकरण, प ूँजी विन्यास, शेयर ं का अंतरण और समामेलि) विवियम, 2024 में प्रस्तावित संश िि ं संबंिी एक्सप ज़र प्रारूप** 

**Exposure Draft on Proposed Amendments to the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (Registration, Capital Structure, Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers) Regulations, 2024** 

दिन ांक/Date: 15.06.2026 

# **1. कायाकारी सारांश** 

सबक  बीम  सबकी रक्ष  (बीम  दिदियोां क  सांशोिन) अदिदनयम, 2025 (“एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम”) के द्व र  ल गू दकये गये पररिर्तनोां के आलोक में भ रर्ीय बीम  दिदनय मक और दिक स प्र दिकरण (आईआरडीएआई), भ रर्ीय बीम  दिदनय मक और दिक स प्र दिकरण (बीम कर् तओां क  पांजीकरण, पजी दिन्य स, शेयरोां क  अांर्रण और सम मेलन) दिदनयम, 2024 (“पांजीकरण दिदनयम”) में सांशोिन करने क  प्रस्त ि करर्  है। 

प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन प दलसीि रकोां के दहर्ोां के सांरक्षण की दनरांर्रर्  को सुदनदिर् करने के स थ ही, बीम अदिदनयम, 1938 के सांशोदिर् उपबांिोां के स थ िर्तम न दिदनय मक ढ ूँचे को सुयोदजर् करने र्थ  एक प रिशी, िृद्धि-अदभमुख, और िैदिक र्ौर पर सुयोदजर् बीम  प ररद्धिदर्क र्ांत्र (ईकोदसस्टम) को सुस ध्य बन ने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं। 

प्रस्त दिर् पररिर्तन दनम्नदलद्धखर् के सांबांि में दिदनय मक ढ ूँचे से सांबांदिर् हैं: 

- भ रर्ीय प्रिर्तकोां और दििेशी प्रिर्तकोां के दलए प त् म निांड, 

- दििेशी दनिेश सांबांिी रक्षोप य, 

- दिशेष प्रयोजन म ध्यम (एसपीिी), 

- शेयरोां के अांर्रण के दलए अनुमोिन, 

- गैर-बीम  कांपदनयोां के स थ बीम  कांपदनयोां क  सम मेलन, 

- आिेिनोां के दलए प्रसांस्करण शुल्क, 

- बीम  कांपदनयोां क  न म, 

- फ मत और आिेिन की प्रदिय एूँ, र्थ 

- अन्य पररण मी और स्पष्टीकरणक री सांशोिन। 

उक्त सांशोिनोां क  उद्देश्य दनम्नदलद्धखर् को समथतन िेन  भी ह 

- व्यिस य करने की सुगमर् , 

- दिदनय मक प्रदिय ओां क  सरलीकरण, 

- अनुप लन व् ोां की कमी, 

- पररच लनगर् स्पष्टर्  की िृद्धि, 

- प जी भरण (कैदपटल इन्फ्यूज़न) क  सुस ध्यीकरण 

प्र दिकरण उक्त प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिनोां पर बीम कर् तओां, प्रिर्तकोां, दनिेशकोां, व्य िस दयक दनक योां, दिदि दिशेषज्ोां, प दलसीि रकोां, और सभी अन्य दहर्ि रकोां से दटप्पदणय ूँ आमांदत् करर्  है। 

# **2. ितामाि विवियामक ढाूँचा और एसबीएसआर अविवियम, 2025 के अंतर्ात वििायी पररिताि** 

भ रर् में बीम कर् तओां के पांजीकरण, पजी दिन्य स, शेयरोां के अांर्रण, सम मेलन और अदभश सन क दनयांत्रण मोटे र्ौर पर बीम  अदिदनयम, 1938 और आईआरडीएआई (बीम कर् तओां क  पांजीकरण, पजी दिन्य स, शेयरोां क  अांर्रण और सम मेलन) दिदनयम, 2024 के द्व र  दकय  ज र्  है। िर्तम न ढ ूँच बीम कर् तओां के पांजीकरण, प्रिर्तकोां की प त् , पजी दिन्य स, शेयरोां के अांर्रण, पजी दलखर्ोां के दनगतम, बीम कर् तओां के सम मेलन, र्थ  योग्य और उदचर् (दफट एण्ड प्रोपर) अपेक्ष ओां से सांबांदिर् व्य पक आिश्यकर् ओां की व्यिि  करर्  है। 

एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम, 2025 ने बीम  क्षेत्र की िृद्धि और दिक स में गदर् ल ने के दलए महत्वपूणत सुि र ल गू दकये हैं।  उक्त सबक  बीम  सबकी रक्ष  (बीम  दिदियोां क  सांशोिन) दििेयक, 2025 (एसबीएसआर दबल) व्यिि  करर्  है दक अथतव्यिि  की बढ़र्ी हुई आिश्यकर् एूँ पूरी करने हेर्ु समथत बन ने के दलए बीम  क्षेत्र की िृद्धि और दिक स में गदर् ल ने के दलए, यह अत्यांर् आिश्यक है दक बीम  दिदियोां में अग्रिशी सुि रोां की एक शांखल  ल गू की ज ए। दिि यी सुि रोां के मुख्य उद्देश्योां में श दमल हैं: 

- बीम  क्षेत्र की िृद्धि और दिक स में आगे और गदर् ल न ; 

- प दलसीि रकोां के बेहर्र सांरक्षण को सुदनदिर् करन ; 

- बीम  कांपदनयोां, मध्यिदर्तयोां और अन्य दहर्ि रकोां के दलए व्यिस य करने की सुगमर्  में सुि र ल न ; 

- दिदनयम बन ने में प रिदशतर्  ल न  र्थ  इस क्षेत्र पर दिदनय मक दनगर नी में सुि र ल न । 

पांजीकरण दिदनयमोां में प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन, उपयुतक्त उद्देश्योां को ध्य न में रखर्े हुए उक्त दिि यी पररिर्तन पररच दलर् करने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं। 

# **3. प्रस्तावित संश िि ं के उद्देश्यः** प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिनोां क  उद्देश्य दनम्नदलद्धखर् हैं: 

- क. उक्त पांजीकरण दिदनयम, 2024 को बीम  अदिदनयम, 1938 के सांशोदिर् उपबांिोां के स थ सुयोदजर् करन ; 

- ख. िोनोां भ रर्ीय और दििेशी प्रिर्तकोां के दलए प्रिर्तक प त् को मजबूर् करन ; 

- ग. पजी भरण को सुस ध्य बन न  और लेनिेनोां की पुनैःसांरचन  करन ; 

- घ. प्रसांस्करण शुल्क और अनुप लन की अपेक्ष ओां को युद्धक्तयुक्त बन न ; 

- ङ. दिदनय मक उपबांिोां की व्य ख्य  में स्पष्टर्  और सुसांगदर् की िृद्धि करन ; 

- च. व्यिस य करने की सुगमर्  क  सांिितन करन ; र्थ 

- छ. गैर-बीम  सांि ओां को सांबि करर्े हुए सम मेलन को सुस ध्य बन न । 

# **4. पंजीकरण विवियम ं में प्रस्तावित संश ििः** मुख्य सांशोिन दनम्न नुस र हैं: 

- **4.1 पररभाषाएूँ और पात्रता मािदंडः** `दििेशी प्रिर्तक’, `भ रर्ीय प्रिर्तक’ और `दिशेष प्रयोजन म ध्यम’ से सांबांदिर् पररभ ष ओां में सांशोिन प्रस्त दिर् हैं। प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन सुसांगदर् को सुदनदिर् करने, अथतदनणतय की अस्पष्टर् ओां को हट ने, दिदनय मक दनगर नी को मजबूर् करने और एफडीआई की सीम  में िृद्धि को िेखर्े हुए रक्षोप य व्यिि एूँ ल गू करने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं। 

- **4.2 पंजीकरण और आिेदि का ढाूँचाः** प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिनोां में दनम्नदलद्धखर् श दमल हैं: 

   - (क) दिदनयमोां में फ मत आईआरडीएआई/आर1 और आईआरडीएआई/आर2 क  अांर्िेशन, 

   - (ख) फ मत आईआरडीएआई/आर3 के दलए अनुसूची सांिभों क  सम िेशन, 

   - (ग) अन पदि प्रम णपत्र (एनओसी) आिेिनोां के दलए प्रसांस्करण शुल्क प्र रांभ करन ; र्थ 

   - (घ) बीम कर् त के न मोां से सांबांदिर् उपबांिोां की पुनैःसांरचन  करन । 

ये सांशोिन आिेिन प्रदिय ओां को औपच ररक रूप िेने, दिदनय मक स्पष्टर्  में सुि र ल ने, र्थ स ांदिदिक अपेक्ष ओां के स थ सुसांगदर् सुदनदिर् करने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं। 

- **4.3 विशेष प्रय जि माध्यम (एसपीिी):** िर्तम न ढ ूँच  दिशेष प्रयोजन म ध्यमोां (एसपीिी) को प्रिर्तक के रूप में क यत करने की अनुमदर् िेर्  है। प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन आिेिकोां से अपेक्ष  करर्  है दक िे दििीय क रति ई क यत-बल (एफएटीएफ) अनुप लनकर् त अदिक र-क्षेत्रोां से प त्र दििेशी दनगदमर् सांि ओां को एसपीिी म न्यर्  िेर्े समय एसपीिी सांरचन ओां की आिश्यकर्  प्रिदशतर् करें। 

- **4.4 शेयर ं का अंतरण और शेयरिाररता अिुम दिः** प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिनोां में दनम्नदलद्धखर् श दमल हैं: 

   - (क) अदिक र दनगतमोां में त्य ग की द्धिदर् में अनुमोिन की प्रयोज्यर्  के सांबांि में स्पष्टीकरण, 

   - (ख) सांशोदिर् ि र  6क प्र रांदभक सीम ओां के स थ सुयोजन। 

   - (ग) स्पष्टीकरण दक अनुमोिन की प्र रांदभक सीम एूँ 5% के प्रत्येक गुणज के दलए ल गू होर्ी हैं, र्थ 

   - (घ) प्रसांस्करण शुल्क क  युद्धक्तकरण। 

ये सांशोिन दिदनयमोां को सांशोदिर् स ांदिदिक उपबांिोां के स थ सुयोदजर् करने, अस्पष्टर् ओां को कम करने र्थ  अनुप लन के ि दयत्वोां को सरल बन ने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं। 

- **4.5 समामेलि ढाूँचा:** एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम गैर-बीम  कांपदनयोां के स थ बीम कर् तओां के सम मेलन को समथत बन र्  है। र्िनुस र, प त्र गैर-बीम  सांि ओां, सम मेलन के दलए शर्ों, प्रदर्फल के भुगर् न की पिदर्, सम मेलन के ब ि के रक्षोप योां, र्थ  प दलसीि रकोां के दहर्ोां के सांरक्षण के दलए उप योां को सद्धिदलर् करर्े हुए एक दिदनय मक ढ ूँच  प्रस्त दिर् है। 

- **4.6 बीमाकतााओं के द्वारा देय शुल्क का युक्तिकरणः** प्र दिकरण प दलसीि रकोां के स्तर पर बीम प्रीदमयम की िहनीयर्  को बढ़ ने एिां बीम  कांपदनयोां की िृद्धि और दिक स को सुदनदिर् करने के दलए भी, बीम कर् तओां के द्व र  प्रबांिन के व् ोां को युद्धक्तयुक्त बन ने पर बल िेर्  रह  है। इसे सुस ध्य बन ने के दलए, बीम कर् तओां के द्व र  िेय दिदभन्न शुल्कोां को युद्धक्तयुक्त बन न  दनम्न नुस र प्रस्त दिर् है: 

   - **समामेलि आिेदि ं के वलए प्रसंस्करण शुल्क:** िर्तम न दिदनयम प्रत्येक बीम कर् त के द्व र  शुल्क क  भुगर् न सकल प्रीदमयम के 1/10िें भ ग की िर से, न्यूनर्म रु. 50 ल ख और अदिकर्म रु. 5 करोड़ के अिीन करने की अपेक्ष  करर्  है, जो प्रत्येक बीम कर् त के द्व र  अलग-अलग िेय है। यह प्रस्त ि है दक उपयुतक्त शुल्क को लेनिेन करनेि ले प्रत्येक पक्षक र के द्व र  िेय, रु. 10 ल ख की दनयर् र दश के रूप में कम दकय  ज ए। 

   - **शेयर ं के अंतरण के आिेदि के वलए प्रसंस्करण शुल्कः** 50% ईदिटी से अदिक अांर्रण की द्धिदर् में, िर्तम न दिदनयम रु. 50 ल ख पर आिेिन शुल्क दनि तररर् करर्  है। इसे रु. 10 ल ख र्क घट ने क  प्रस्त ि है। 

शुल्क में प्रस्त दिर् कमी क  उद्देश्य व्यिस य करने की सुगमर्  को समथतन िेन , अनुप लन व् ोां को घट न , िृद्धि और दनिेश को प्रोत्स दहर् करन , र्थ  एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम के उद्देश्योां के स थ सुयोजन करन  है। 

- **4.7 बीमा कंपविय ं का िामः** एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम ने बीम कर् त के न म के सांबांि में शर्ें प्रिदर्तर् की हैं अथ तर बीम कर् त के न म में शब्द `बीम ’, `आि सन’ (एश्योरेंस) अथि  `पुनबीम ’ दनदहर् होग । इसे ध्य न में रखर्े हुए, बीम कर् तओां के न म के सांबांि में एक नय  दिदनयम 56क प्र रांभ करने क  प्रस्त ि दकय  ज र्  है। यह िर्तम न बीम कर् तओां के दलए पररिर्ी समय-सीम  उपलब्ध कर र्  है र्थ  बीम कर् तओां के न म में पररिर्तन के दलए पूित-अनुमोिन की आिश्यकर्  को ल गू करर्  है। 

**5. व्यिसाय करिे की सुर्मता के उपायः** प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन, व्यिस य करने की सुगमर्  में सुि र ल ने के उद्देश्य से युक्त कई उप य सम दिष्ट करर्े हैं, दजनमें दनम्नदलद्धखर् श दमल हैं:

# **1. Executive Summary** 

The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) proposes amendments to the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (Registration, Capital Structure, Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers) Regulations, 2024 (“Registration Regulations”), in light of the changes introduced through the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025 (“SBSR Act”). 

The proposed amendments seek to align the existing regulatory framework with the revised provisions of the Insurance Act, 1938 and facilitate a transparent, growthoriented, and globally aligned insurance ecosystem while ensuring continued protection of policyholder interests. 

The proposed changes relate to regulatory framework regarding: 

- Eligibility criteria for Indian Promoters and Foreign Promoters, 

- Foreign investment safeguards, 

- Special purpose vehicles (SPVs), 

- Approval for Transfer of shares, 

- Amalgamation of insurance companies with non-insurance companies, 

- Processing fees for applications, 

- Name of insurance companies, 

- Forms and application procedures, and 

- Other consequential and clarificatory amendments. 

The amendments are also intended to support: 

- Ease of doing business, 

- Simplification of regulatory processes, 

- Reduction of compliance costs, 

- Enhancement of operational clarity, 

- Facilitation of capital infusion. 

The Authority invites comments from insurers, promoters, investors, professional bodies, legal experts, policyholders, and all other stakeholders on the proposed amendments. 

# **2. Existing Regulatory Framework and Legislative Changes under the SBSR Act, 2025** 

The registration, capital structure, transfer of shares, amalgamation and governance of insurers in India are broadly governed by the Insurance Act, 1938 and the IRDAI (Registration, Capital Structure, Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers) Regulations, 2024. The existing framework provides comprehensive requirements relating to Registration of insurers, Promoter eligibility, Capital structure, Transfer of shares, Issuance of capital instruments, Amalgamation of insurers, and Fit and proper requirements. 

The SBSR Act, 2025 introduced significant reforms to accelerate the growth and development of the insurance sector. The statement of objects and reasons as per The Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Bill, 2025 (SBSR Bill) provides that in order to accelerate the growth and development of the insurance sector for enabling it to cater to the growing needs of the economy, it is essential to introduce a series of forward-looking reforms in the insurance laws. The key objectives of the legislative reforms include: 

- To further accelerate the growth and development of the insurance sector; 

- To ensure better protection of policyholders; 

- To improve ease of doing business for insurance companies, intermediaries and other stakeholders; 

- To bring transparency in regulation making and to improve regulatory oversight over the sector. 

The proposed amendments to the Registration Regulations seek to operationalise the legislative changes keeping in view the above mentioned objectives. 

# **3. Objectives of the Proposed Amendments:** The proposed amendments aim to: 

- a. Align the Registration Regulations 2024 with the amended provisions of the Insurance Act, 1938; 

- b. Strengthen promoter eligibility for both Indian and Foreign Promoters. 

- c. Facilitate capital infusion and restructuring transactions; 

- d. Rationalise processing fees and compliance requirements; 

- e. Enhance clarity and consistency in interpretation of regulatory provisions; 

- f. Promote ease of doing business; and 

- g. Facilitate amalgamation involving non-insurance entities. 

**4. Proposed Amendments to Registration Regulations:** Key Amendments are as under: 

**4.1 Definitions and Eligibility Criteria:** Amendments are proposed in definitions relating to ‘Foreign Promoter’, ‘Indian Promoter’ and ‘Special Purpose Vehicle’. The proposed amendments seek to ensure consistency, remove interpretational ambiguities, strengthen regulatory oversight and put in place safeguard mechanisms in view of increase in limit of FDI. 

# **4.2 Registration and Application Framework:** The proposed amendments include: 

- (a) Insertion of Forms IRDAI/R1 and IRDAI/R2 into the Regulations, 

- (b) Inclusion of Schedule references for Form IRDAI/R3, 

- (c) Introduction of processing fees for No Objection Certificate (NOC) applications; and 

- (d) Restructuring of provisions relating to insurer names. 

These amendments seek to formalise application procedures, improve regulatory clarity and ensure consistency with statutory requirements. 

**4.3 Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs):** The existing framework permits SPVs to act as promoter. The proposed amendment requires applicants to demonstrate the necessity of SPV structures while extending SPV recognition to eligible foreign incorporated entities from Financial Action Task Force (FATF) compliant jurisdictions. 

**4.4 Transfer of Shares and Shareholding Approvals:** The proposed amendments include: 

- (a) Clarification regarding applicability of approval in case of renunciation in rights issues, 

- (b) Alignment with revised Section 6A thresholds. 

- (c) Clarification that approval thresholds apply for every multiple of 5%, and 

- (d) Rationalisation of processing fees. 

These amendments seek to align the Regulations with amended statutory provisions, reduce ambiguities and simplify compliance obligations. 

**4.5 Amalgamation Framework:** The SBSR Act enables amalgamation of insurers with non-insurance companies. Accordingly, a regulatory framework is proposed covering eligible non-insurance entities, conditions for amalgamation, manner of payment of consideration, post-amalgamation safeguards, and measures for protection of policyholder interests. 

**4.6 Rationalisation of Fees Payable by Insurers:** The Authority has been emphasising on rationalising the expenses of management by the insurers to increase affordability of insurance premium at the end of policyholders and also to ensure growth and development of insurance companies as well. To facilitate the same, it is proposed to rationalise various fees payable by insurers, as under: 

- **Processing fee for Amalgamation Applications:** Current regulations requires payment of fee by each of the insurer at the rate of 1/10<sup>th</sup> of Gross Premium, subject to minimum of Rs.50 lakh and maximum of Rs.5 crores, payable separately by each insurer. It is proposed to reduce the said fees to a fixed amount of Rs.10 lakh, payable by each of the transacting parties. 

- **Processing fee for Transfer of Shares Application:** In case of transfer of more than 50% equity, the existing regulations stipulates application fee at Rs.50 lakh. The same is proposed to be reduced to Rs.10 lakh. 

The proposed reduction in fees is intended to support ease of doing business, reduce compliance costs, encourage growth and investment, and align with the objectives of the SBSR Act. 

**4.7 Name of Insurance Companies:** The SBSR Act has introduced stipulations with regard to the name of the insurer i.e. the name of the insurer shall contain ‘insurance’, ‘assurance’ or ‘reinsurance’ word in it. In view of the same, it is proposed to introduce 

a new Regulation 56A relating to name of insurers. It also provides the transitional timeline for existing insurers and prior approval requirement for change in name of insurers. 

**5. Ease of Doing Business Measures:** The proposed amendments incorporate several measures aimed at improving ease of doing business, including: 

- Reduction in processing fees for amalgamation applications and processing fees for transfer of shares applications. 

- Removal of valuation certification requirements at the registration stage. 

- Providing clarity with regard to approval requirements for issuance of shares at premium, and 

- Formalisation of registration application procedures. 

These measures are expected to reduce operational burden, facilitate investment, support restructuring transactions and enhance efficiency within the insurance sector. 

**6. Protection of Policyholder Interests and Regulatory Safeguards:** While facilitating ease of doing business and enhanced investment, the proposed amendments continue to prioritise protection of policyholder interests. The proposed safeguards include: 

- Restricting permissible foreign promoters to (i) incorporated entities (ii) from FATF compliant jurisdictions. 

- Introducing safeguards for amalgamation transactions involving non-insurance companies including restriction on utilisation of policyholder’s fund for the payment of consideration. 

**7. Stakeholder Feedback:** The Authority invites comments from stakeholders on the proposed amendments. Exposure draft of the Regulation is as per Annexure 1. Stakeholders may submit their comments on the Exposure Draft in the format specified in Annexure 2. Feedback may be submitted to Mr. Nirmal Jain at <u>nirmal.jain@irdai.gov.in</u> with a copy to Mr. Mahesh Agarwal at <u>maheshagarwal@irdai.gov.in on or before 6</u><sup>th</sup> July, 2026. 

**This Consultation Paper is issued for public and industry comments.**

- **4.2 पंजीकरण और आिेदि का ढाूँचाः** प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिनोां में दनम्नदलद्धखर् श दमल हैं:

   - (ख) फ मत आईआरडीएआई/आर3 के दलए अनुसूची सांिभों क  सम िेशन, 

   - (ग) अन पदि प्रम णपत्र (एनओसी) आिेिनोां के दलए प्रसांस्करण शुल्क प्र रांभ करन ; र्थ

ये सांशोिन आिेिन प्रदिय ओां को औपच ररक रूप िेने, दिदनय मक स्पष्टर्  में सुि र ल ने, र्थ स ांदिदिक अपेक्ष ओां के स थ सुसांगदर् सुदनदिर् करने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं। 

- **4.3 विशेष प्रय जि माध्यम (एसपीिी):** िर्तम न ढ ूँच  दिशेष प्रयोजन म ध्यमोां (एसपीिी) को प्रिर्तक के रूप में क यत करने की अनुमदर् िेर्  है। प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन आिेिकोां से अपेक्ष  करर्  है दक िे दििीय क रति ई क यत-बल (एफएटीएफ) अनुप लनकर् त अदिक र-क्षेत्रोां से प त्र दििेशी दनगदमर् सांि ओां को एसपीिी म न्यर्  िेर्े समय एसपीिी सांरचन ओां की आिश्यकर्  प्रिदशतर् करें।

   - (क) अदिक र दनगतमोां में त्य ग की द्धिदर् में अनुमोिन की प्रयोज्यर्  के सांबांि में स्पष्टीकरण, 

   - (ख) सांशोदिर् ि र  6क प्र रांदभक सीम ओां के स थ सुयोजन।

   - (घ) प्रसांस्करण शुल्क क  युद्धक्तकरण। 

ये सांशोिन दिदनयमोां को सांशोदिर् स ांदिदिक उपबांिोां के स थ सुयोदजर् करने, अस्पष्टर् ओां को कम करने र्थ  अनुप लन के ि दयत्वोां को सरल बन ने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं।

- **4.6 बीमाकतााओं के द्वारा देय शुल्क का युक्तिकरणः** प्र दिकरण प दलसीि रकोां के स्तर पर बीम प्रीदमयम की िहनीयर्  को बढ़ ने एिां बीम  कांपदनयोां की िृद्धि और दिक स को सुदनदिर् करने के दलए भी, बीम कर् तओां के द्व र  प्रबांिन के व् ोां को युद्धक्तयुक्त बन ने पर बल िेर्  रह  है। इसे सुस ध्य बन ने के दलए, बीम कर् तओां के द्व र  िेय दिदभन्न शुल्कोां को युद्धक्तयुक्त बन न  दनम्न नुस र प्रस्त दिर् है: 

   - **समामेलि आिेदि ं के वलए प्रसंस्करण शुल्क:** िर्तम न दिदनयम प्रत्येक बीम कर् त के द्व र  शुल्क क  भुगर् न सकल प्रीदमयम के 1/10िें भ ग की िर से, न्यूनर्म रु. 50 ल ख और अदिकर्म रु. 5 करोड़ के अिीन करने की अपेक्ष  करर्  है, जो प्रत्येक बीम कर् त के द्व र  अलग-अलग िेय है। यह प्रस्त ि है दक उपयुतक्त शुल्क को लेनिेन करनेि ले प्रत्येक पक्षक र के द्व र  िेय, रु. 10 ल ख की दनयर् र दश के रूप में कम दकय  ज ए। 

   - **शेयर ं के अंतरण के आिेदि के वलए प्रसंस्करण शुल्कः** 50% ईदिटी से अदिक अांर्रण की द्धिदर् में, िर्तम न दिदनयम रु. 50 ल ख पर आिेिन शुल्क दनि तररर् करर्  है। इसे रु. 10 ल ख र्क घट ने क  प्रस्त ि है। 

शुल्क में प्रस्त दिर् कमी क  उद्देश्य व्यिस य करने की सुगमर्  को समथतन िेन , अनुप लन व् ोां को घट न , िृद्धि और दनिेश को प्रोत्स दहर् करन , र्थ  एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम के उद्देश्योां के स थ सुयोजन करन  है। 

- **4.7 बीमा कंपविय ं का िामः** एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम ने बीम कर् त के न म के सांबांि में शर्ें प्रिदर्तर् की हैं अथ तर बीम कर् त के न म में शब्द `बीम ’, `आि सन’ (एश्योरेंस) अथि  `पुनबीम ’ दनदहर् होग । इसे ध्य न में रखर्े हुए, बीम कर् तओां के न म के सांबांि में एक नय  दिदनयम 56क प्र रांभ करने क  प्रस्त ि दकय  ज र्  है। यह िर्तम न बीम कर् तओां के दलए पररिर्ी समय-सीम  उपलब्ध कर र्  है र्थ  बीम कर् तओां के न म में पररिर्तन के दलए पूित-अनुमोिन की आिश्यकर्  को ल गू करर्  है। 

**5. व्यिसाय करिे की सुर्मता के उपायः** प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन, व्यिस य करने की सुगमर्  में सुि र ल ने के उद्देश्य से युक्त कई उप य सम दिष्ट करर्े हैं, दजनमें दनम्नदलद्धखर् श दमल हैं: 

- सम मेलन आिेिनोां के दलए प्रसांस्करण शुल्क में र्थ  शेयरोां के अांर्रण आिेिनोां के दलए प्रसांस्करण शुल्क में कमी। 

- पांजीकरण के स्तर पर मूल् ांकन प्रम णीकरण की आिश्यकर् एूँ हट न । 

- प्रीदमयम पर शेयरोां के दनगतम के दलए अनुमोिन की आिश्यकर् ओां के सांबांि में स्पष्टर्  प्रि न करन , र्थ 

- पांजीकरण आिेिन प्रदिय ओां को औपच ररक रूप िेन । 

यह प्रत्य दशर् है दक ये उप य पररच लनगर् भ र को कम करेंगे, दनिेश को सुस ध्य बन एूँगे, पुनैःसांरचन सांबांिी लेनिेनोां को समथतन िेंगे र्थ  बीम  क्षेत्र के अांिर क यतकुशलर्  क  सांिितन करेंगे। 

**6. पावलसीिारक ं के वित ं का संरक्षण और विवियामक रक्ष पायः** व्यिस य को सुगम बन ने और दनिेश के सांिितन में सह यर्  करने के स थ ही, प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन प दलसीि रकोां के दहर्ोां के सांरक्षण को प्र थदमकर्  िेन  ज री रखर्े हैं। प्रस्त दिर् रक्षोप योां में श दमल हैं: 

- अनुमदर्योग्य दििेशी प्रिर्तकोां को (i) दनगदमर् सांि ओां (ii) एफएटीएफ अनुप लनकर् त अदिक र-क्षेत्रोां से आनेि लोां र्क सीदमर् रखन । 

- प्रदर्फल के भुगर् न के दलए प दलसीि रकोां की दनदि के उपयोग पर प्रदर्बांि सदहर्, गैर-बीम कांपदनयोां को सांबि करर्े हुए सम मेलन सांबांिी लेनिेनोां के दलए रक्षोप य प्र रांभ करन । 

**7. वितिारक ं की प्रवतस चिा (फीडबैक):** प्र दिकरण प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिनोां पर दहर्ि रकोां से दटप्पदणय ूँ आमांदत् करर्  है। दिदनयम क  एक्सपोज़र प्र रूप अनुबांि 1 के अनुस र है। दहर्ि रक उक्त एक्सपोज़र प्र रूप पर अपनी दटप्पदणय ूँ अनुबांि 2 में दिदनदितष्ट फ मेट में प्रस्तुर् कर सकर्े हैं। प्रदर्सूचन  (फीडबैक) श्री दनमतल जैन को nirmal.jain@irdai.gov.in पर प्रस्तुर् दकय  ज ए र्थ  उसकी एक प्रदर् श्री महेश अग्रि ल को <u>maheshagarwal@irdai.gov.in को 6 जुल ई, 2026 को य  उससे पहले प्रेदषर् की ज ए।</u> 

**यि परामशा-पत्र जिता और उद्य र् की विप्पवणय ं के वलए जारी वकया जाता िै।** 

# **1. Executive Summary** 

The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) proposes amendments to the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (Registration, Capital Structure, Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers) Regulations, 2024 (“Registration Regulations”), in light of the changes introduced through the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025 (“SBSR Act”). 

The proposed amendments seek to align the existing regulatory framework with the revised provisions of the Insurance Act, 1938 and facilitate a transparent, growthoriented, and globally aligned insurance ecosystem while ensuring continued protection of policyholder interests. 

The proposed changes relate to regulatory framework regarding: 

- Eligibility criteria for Indian Promoters and Foreign Promoters, 

- Foreign investment safeguards, 

- Special purpose vehicles (SPVs), 

- Approval for Transfer of shares, 

- Amalgamation of insurance companies with non-insurance companies, 

- Processing fees for applications, 

- Name of insurance companies, 

- Forms and application procedures, and 

- Other consequential and clarificatory amendments. 

The amendments are also intended to support: 

- Ease of doing business, 

- Simplification of regulatory processes, 

- Reduction of compliance costs, 

- Enhancement of operational clarity, 

- Facilitation of capital infusion. 

The Authority invites comments from insurers, promoters, investors, professional bodies, legal experts, policyholders, and all other stakeholders on the proposed amendments. 

# **2. Existing Regulatory Framework and Legislative Changes under the SBSR Act, 2025** 

The registration, capital structure, transfer of shares, amalgamation and governance of insurers in India are broadly governed by the Insurance Act, 1938 and the IRDAI (Registration, Capital Structure, Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers) Regulations, 2024. The existing framework provides comprehensive requirements relating to Registration of insurers, Promoter eligibility, Capital structure, Transfer of shares, Issuance of capital instruments, Amalgamation of insurers, and Fit and proper requirements. 

The SBSR Act, 2025 introduced significant reforms to accelerate the growth and development of the insurance sector. The statement of objects and reasons as per The Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Bill, 2025 (SBSR Bill) provides that in order to accelerate the growth and development of the insurance sector for enabling it to cater to the growing needs of the economy, it is essential to introduce a series of forward-looking reforms in the insurance laws. The key objectives of the legislative reforms include: 

- To further accelerate the growth and development of the insurance sector; 

- To ensure better protection of policyholders; 

- To improve ease of doing business for insurance companies, intermediaries and other stakeholders; 

- To bring transparency in regulation making and to improve regulatory oversight over the sector. 

The proposed amendments to the Registration Regulations seek to operationalise the legislative changes keeping in view the above mentioned objectives. 

# **3. Objectives of the Proposed Amendments:** The proposed amendments aim to: 

- a. Align the Registration Regulations 2024 with the amended provisions of the Insurance Act, 1938; 

- b. Strengthen promoter eligibility for both Indian and Foreign Promoters. 

- c. Facilitate capital infusion and restructuring transactions; 

- d. Rationalise processing fees and compliance requirements; 

- e. Enhance clarity and consistency in interpretation of regulatory provisions; 

- f. Promote ease of doing business; and 

- g. Facilitate amalgamation involving non-insurance entities. 

**4. Proposed Amendments to Registration Regulations:** Key Amendments are as under: 

**4.1 Definitions and Eligibility Criteria:** Amendments are proposed in definitions relating to ‘Foreign Promoter’, ‘Indian Promoter’ and ‘Special Purpose Vehicle’. The proposed amendments seek to ensure consistency, remove interpretational ambiguities, strengthen regulatory oversight and put in place safeguard mechanisms in view of increase in limit of FDI. 

# **4.2 Registration and Application Framework:** The proposed amendments include: 

- (a) Insertion of Forms IRDAI/R1 and IRDAI/R2 into the Regulations, 

- (b) Inclusion of Schedule references for Form IRDAI/R3, 

- (c) Introduction of processing fees for No Objection Certificate (NOC) applications; and 

- (d) Restructuring of provisions relating to insurer names. 

These amendments seek to formalise application procedures, improve regulatory clarity and ensure consistency with statutory requirements. 

**4.3 Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs):** The existing framework permits SPVs to act as promoter. The proposed amendment requires applicants to demonstrate the necessity of SPV structures while extending SPV recognition to eligible foreign incorporated entities from Financial Action Task Force (FATF) compliant jurisdictions. 

**4.4 Transfer of Shares and Shareholding Approvals:** The proposed amendments include: 

- (a) Clarification regarding applicability of approval in case of renunciation in rights issues, 

- (b) Alignment with revised Section 6A thresholds. 

- (c) Clarification that approval thresholds apply for every multiple of 5%, and 

- (d) Rationalisation of processing fees. 

These amendments seek to align the Regulations with amended statutory provisions, reduce ambiguities and simplify compliance obligations. 

**4.5 Amalgamation Framework:** The SBSR Act enables amalgamation of insurers with non-insurance companies. Accordingly, a regulatory framework is proposed covering eligible non-insurance entities, conditions for amalgamation, manner of payment of consideration, post-amalgamation safeguards, and measures for protection of policyholder interests. 

**4.6 Rationalisation of Fees Payable by Insurers:** The Authority has been emphasising on rationalising the expenses of management by the insurers to increase affordability of insurance premium at the end of policyholders and also to ensure growth and development of insurance companies as well. To facilitate the same, it is proposed to rationalise various fees payable by insurers, as under: 

- **Processing fee for Amalgamation Applications:** Current regulations requires payment of fee by each of the insurer at the rate of 1/10<sup>th</sup> of Gross Premium, subject to minimum of Rs.50 lakh and maximum of Rs.5 crores, payable separately by each insurer. It is proposed to reduce the said fees to a fixed amount of Rs.10 lakh, payable by each of the transacting parties. 

- **Processing fee for Transfer of Shares Application:** In case of transfer of more than 50% equity, the existing regulations stipulates application fee at Rs.50 lakh. The same is proposed to be reduced to Rs.10 lakh. 

The proposed reduction in fees is intended to support ease of doing business, reduce compliance costs, encourage growth and investment, and align with the objectives of the SBSR Act. 

**4.7 Name of Insurance Companies:** The SBSR Act has introduced stipulations with regard to the name of the insurer i.e. the name of the insurer shall contain ‘insurance’, ‘assurance’ or ‘reinsurance’ word in it. In view of the same, it is proposed to introduce 

a new Regulation 56A relating to name of insurers. It also provides the transitional timeline for existing insurers and prior approval requirement for change in name of insurers. 

**5. Ease of Doing Business Measures:** The proposed amendments incorporate several measures aimed at improving ease of doing business, including: 

- Reduction in processing fees for amalgamation applications and processing fees for transfer of shares applications. 

- Removal of valuation certification requirements at the registration stage. 

- Providing clarity with regard to approval requirements for issuance of shares at premium, and 

- Formalisation of registration application procedures. 

These measures are expected to reduce operational burden, facilitate investment, support restructuring transactions and enhance efficiency within the insurance sector. 

**6. Protection of Policyholder Interests and Regulatory Safeguards:** While facilitating ease of doing business and enhanced investment, the proposed amendments continue to prioritise protection of policyholder interests. The proposed safeguards include: 

- Restricting permissible foreign promoters to (i) incorporated entities (ii) from FATF compliant jurisdictions. 

- Introducing safeguards for amalgamation transactions involving non-insurance companies including restriction on utilisation of policyholder’s fund for the payment of consideration. 

**7. Stakeholder Feedback:** The Authority invites comments from stakeholders on the proposed amendments. Exposure draft of the Regulation is as per Annexure 1. Stakeholders may submit their comments on the Exposure Draft in the format specified in Annexure 2. Feedback may be submitted to Mr. Nirmal Jain at <u>nirmal.jain@irdai.gov.in</u> with a copy to Mr. Mahesh Agarwal at <u>maheshagarwal@irdai.gov.in on or before 6</u><sup>th</sup> July, 2026. 

**This Consultation Paper is issued for public and industry comments.**

- **4.1 पररभाषाएूँ और पात्रता मािदंडः** `दििेशी प्रिर्तक’, `भ रर्ीय प्रिर्तक’ और `दिशेष प्रयोजन म ध्यम’ से सांबांदिर् पररभ ष ओां में सांशोिन प्रस्त दिर् हैं। प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन सुसांगदर् को सुदनदिर् करने, अथतदनणतय की अस्पष्टर् ओां को हट ने, दिदनय मक दनगर नी को मजबूर् करने और एफडीआई की सीम  में िृद्धि को िेखर्े हुए रक्षोप य व्यिि एूँ ल गू करने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं। 

- **4.2 पंजीकरण और आिेदि का ढाूँचाः** प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिनोां में दनम्नदलद्धखर् श दमल हैं: 

   - (क) दिदनयमोां में फ मत आईआरडीएआई/आर1 और आईआरडीएआई/आर2 क  अांर्िेशन, 

   - (ख) फ मत आईआरडीएआई/आर3 के दलए अनुसूची सांिभों क  सम िेशन, 

   - (ग) अन पदि प्रम णपत्र (एनओसी) आिेिनोां के दलए प्रसांस्करण शुल्क प्र रांभ करन ; र्थ 

   - (घ) बीम कर् त के न मोां से सांबांदिर् उपबांिोां की पुनैःसांरचन  करन । 

ये सांशोिन आिेिन प्रदिय ओां को औपच ररक रूप िेने, दिदनय मक स्पष्टर्  में सुि र ल ने, र्थ स ांदिदिक अपेक्ष ओां के स थ सुसांगदर् सुदनदिर् करने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं। 

- **4.3 विशेष प्रय जि माध्यम (एसपीिी):** िर्तम न ढ ूँच  दिशेष प्रयोजन म ध्यमोां (एसपीिी) को प्रिर्तक के रूप में क यत करने की अनुमदर् िेर्  है। प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिन आिेिकोां से अपेक्ष  करर्  है दक िे दििीय क रति ई क यत-बल (एफएटीएफ) अनुप लनकर् त अदिक र-क्षेत्रोां से प त्र दििेशी दनगदमर् सांि ओां को एसपीिी म न्यर्  िेर्े समय एसपीिी सांरचन ओां की आिश्यकर्  प्रिदशतर् करें। 

- **4.4 शेयर ं का अंतरण और शेयरिाररता अिुम दिः** प्रस्त दिर् सांशोिनोां में दनम्नदलद्धखर् श दमल हैं: 

   - (क) अदिक र दनगतमोां में त्य ग की द्धिदर् में अनुमोिन की प्रयोज्यर्  के सांबांि में स्पष्टीकरण, 

   - (ख) सांशोदिर् ि र  6क प्र रांदभक सीम ओां के स थ सुयोजन। 

   - (ग) स्पष्टीकरण दक अनुमोिन की प्र रांदभक सीम एूँ 5% के प्रत्येक गुणज के दलए ल गू होर्ी हैं, र्थ 

   - (घ) प्रसांस्करण शुल्क क  युद्धक्तकरण। 

ये सांशोिन दिदनयमोां को सांशोदिर् स ांदिदिक उपबांिोां के स थ सुयोदजर् करने, अस्पष्टर् ओां को कम करने र्थ  अनुप लन के ि दयत्वोां को सरल बन ने की अपेक्ष  करर्े हैं।

- **4.5 समामेलि ढाूँचा:** एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम गैर-बीम  कांपदनयोां के स थ बीम कर् तओां के सम मेलन को समथत बन र्  है। र्िनुस र, प त्र गैर-बीम  सांि ओां, सम मेलन के दलए शर्ों, प्रदर्फल के भुगर् न की पिदर्, सम मेलन के ब ि के रक्षोप योां, र्थ  प दलसीि रकोां के दहर्ोां के सांरक्षण के दलए उप योां को सद्धिदलर् करर्े हुए एक दिदनय मक ढ ूँच  प्रस्त दिर् है। 

- **4.6 बीमाकतााओं के द्वारा देय शुल्क का युक्तिकरणः** प्र दिकरण प दलसीि रकोां के स्तर पर बीम प्रीदमयम की िहनीयर्  को बढ़ ने एिां बीम  कांपदनयोां की िृद्धि और दिक स को सुदनदिर् करने के दलए भी, बीम कर् तओां के द्व र  प्रबांिन के व् ोां को युद्धक्तयुक्त बन ने पर बल िेर्  रह  है। इसे सुस ध्य बन ने के दलए, बीम कर् तओां के द्व र  िेय दिदभन्न शुल्कोां को युद्धक्तयुक्त बन न  दनम्न नुस र प्रस्त दिर् है: 

   - **समामेलि आिेदि ं के वलए प्रसंस्करण शुल्क:** िर्तम न दिदनयम प्रत्येक बीम कर् त के द्व र  शुल्क क  भुगर् न सकल प्रीदमयम के 1/10िें भ ग की िर से, न्यूनर्म रु. 50 ल ख और अदिकर्म रु. 5 करोड़ के अिीन करने की अपेक्ष  करर्  है, जो प्रत्येक बीम कर् त के द्व र  अलग-अलग िेय है। यह प्रस्त ि है दक उपयुतक्त शुल्क को लेनिेन करनेि ले प्रत्येक पक्षक र के द्व र  िेय, रु. 10 ल ख की दनयर् र दश के रूप में कम दकय  ज ए। 

   - **शेयर ं के अंतरण के आिेदि के वलए प्रसंस्करण शुल्कः** 50% ईदिटी से अदिक अांर्रण की द्धिदर् में, िर्तम न दिदनयम रु. 50 ल ख पर आिेिन शुल्क दनि तररर् करर्  है। इसे रु. 10 ल ख र्क घट ने क  प्रस्त ि है। 

शुल्क में प्रस्त दिर् कमी क  उद्देश्य व्यिस य करने की सुगमर्  को समथतन िेन , अनुप लन व् ोां को घट न , िृद्धि और दनिेश को प्रोत्स दहर् करन , र्थ  एसबीएसआर अदिदनयम के उद्देश्योां के स थ सुयोजन करन  है।

chunks.heading: **1. Executive Summary**
chunks.attachment: परामर्श दस्तावेज़-आईआरडीएआई(बीमाकर्ताओं का पंजीकरण,पूँजी विन्यास,शेयरों का अंतरण और समामेलन) (प्रथम संशोधन),विनियम,2026_Consultation Paper-IRDAI(Registration,Capital Structure,Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers)(1st Amendment)Regulations,.pdf
chunks.content:
The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) proposes amendments to the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (Registration, Capital Structure, Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers) Regulations, 2024 (“Registration Regulations”), in light of the changes introduced through the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025 (“SBSR Act”). 

The proposed amendments seek to align the existing regulatory framework with the revised provisions of the Insurance Act, 1938 and facilitate a transparent, growthoriented, and globally aligned insurance ecosystem while ensuring continued protection of policyholder interests. 

The proposed changes relate to regulatory framework regarding: 

- Eligibility criteria for Indian Promoters and Foreign Promoters, 

- Foreign investment safeguards, 

- Special purpose vehicles (SPVs), 

- Approval for Transfer of shares, 

- Amalgamation of insurance companies with non-insurance companies, 

- Processing fees for applications, 

- Name of insurance companies, 

- Forms and application procedures, and 

- Other consequential and clarificatory amendments. 

The amendments are also intended to support: 

- Ease of doing business, 

- Simplification of regulatory processes, 

- Reduction of compliance costs, 

- Enhancement of operational clarity, 

- Facilitation of capital infusion. 

The Authority invites comments from insurers, promoters, investors, professional bodies, legal experts, policyholders, and all other stakeholders on the proposed amendments.

chunks.heading: **2. Existing Regulatory Framework and Legislative Changes under the SBSR Act, 2025**
chunks.attachment: परामर्श दस्तावेज़-आईआरडीएआई(बीमाकर्ताओं का पंजीकरण,पूँजी विन्यास,शेयरों का अंतरण और समामेलन) (प्रथम संशोधन),विनियम,2026_Consultation Paper-IRDAI(Registration,Capital Structure,Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers)(1st Amendment)Regulations,.pdf

chunks.heading: **3. Objectives of the Proposed Amendments:** The proposed amendments aim to:
chunks.attachment: परामर्श दस्तावेज़-आईआरडीएआई(बीमाकर्ताओं का पंजीकरण,पूँजी विन्यास,शेयरों का अंतरण और समामेलन) (प्रथम संशोधन),विनियम,2026_Consultation Paper-IRDAI(Registration,Capital Structure,Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers)(1st Amendment)Regulations,.pdf

chunks.heading: 4. Proposed Amendments to Registration Regulations:
chunks.attachment: परामर्श दस्तावेज़-आईआरडीएआई(बीमाकर्ताओं का पंजीकरण,पूँजी विन्यास,शेयरों का अंतरण और समामेलन) (प्रथम संशोधन),विनियम,2026_Consultation Paper-IRDAI(Registration,Capital Structure,Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers)(1st Amendment)Regulations,.pdf
chunks.content:
Key Amendments are as under: 

**4.1 Definitions and Eligibility Criteria:** Amendments are proposed in definitions relating to ‘Foreign Promoter’, ‘Indian Promoter’ and ‘Special Purpose Vehicle’. The proposed amendments seek to ensure consistency, remove interpretational ambiguities, strengthen regulatory oversight and put in place safeguard mechanisms in view of increase in limit of FDI.

chunks.heading: **4.2 Registration and Application Framework:** The proposed amendments include:
chunks.attachment: परामर्श दस्तावेज़-आईआरडीएआई(बीमाकर्ताओं का पंजीकरण,पूँजी विन्यास,शेयरों का अंतरण और समामेलन) (प्रथम संशोधन),विनियम,2026_Consultation Paper-IRDAI(Registration,Capital Structure,Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers)(1st Amendment)Regulations,.pdf
chunks.content:
- (a) Insertion of Forms IRDAI/R1 and IRDAI/R2 into the Regulations, 

- (b) Inclusion of Schedule references for Form IRDAI/R3, 

- (c) Introduction of processing fees for No Objection Certificate (NOC) applications; and 

- (d) Restructuring of provisions relating to insurer names. 

These amendments seek to formalise application procedures, improve regulatory clarity and ensure consistency with statutory requirements. 

**4.3 Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs):** The existing framework permits SPVs to act as promoter. The proposed amendment requires applicants to demonstrate the necessity of SPV structures while extending SPV recognition to eligible foreign incorporated entities from Financial Action Task Force (FATF) compliant jurisdictions. 

**4.4 Transfer of Shares and Shareholding Approvals:** The proposed amendments include: 

- (a) Clarification regarding applicability of approval in case of renunciation in rights issues, 

- (b) Alignment with revised Section 6A thresholds. 

- (c) Clarification that approval thresholds apply for every multiple of 5%, and 

- (d) Rationalisation of processing fees. 

These amendments seek to align the Regulations with amended statutory provisions, reduce ambiguities and simplify compliance obligations. 

**4.5 Amalgamation Framework:** The SBSR Act enables amalgamation of insurers with non-insurance companies. Accordingly, a regulatory framework is proposed covering eligible non-insurance entities, conditions for amalgamation, manner of payment of consideration, post-amalgamation safeguards, and measures for protection of policyholder interests. 

**4.6 Rationalisation of Fees Payable by Insurers:** The Authority has been emphasising on rationalising the expenses of management by the insurers to increase affordability of insurance premium at the end of policyholders and also to ensure growth and development of insurance companies as well.

chunks.heading: **4.2 Registration and Application Framework:** The proposed amendments include:
chunks.attachment: परामर्श दस्तावेज़-आईआरडीएआई(बीमाकर्ताओं का पंजीकरण,पूँजी विन्यास,शेयरों का अंतरण और समामेलन) (प्रथम संशोधन),विनियम,2026_Consultation Paper-IRDAI(Registration,Capital Structure,Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers)(1st Amendment)Regulations,.pdf
chunks.content:
**4.6 Rationalisation of Fees Payable by Insurers:** The Authority has been emphasising on rationalising the expenses of management by the insurers to increase affordability of insurance premium at the end of policyholders and also to ensure growth and development of insurance companies as well. To facilitate the same, it is proposed to rationalise various fees payable by insurers, as under: 

- **Processing fee for Amalgamation Applications:** Current regulations requires payment of fee by each of the insurer at the rate of 1/10<sup>th</sup> of Gross Premium, subject to minimum of Rs.50 lakh and maximum of Rs.5 crores, payable separately by each insurer. It is proposed to reduce the said fees to a fixed amount of Rs.10 lakh, payable by each of the transacting parties. 

- **Processing fee for Transfer of Shares Application:** In case of transfer of more than 50% equity, the existing regulations stipulates application fee at Rs.50 lakh. The same is proposed to be reduced to Rs.10 lakh. 

The proposed reduction in fees is intended to support ease of doing business, reduce compliance costs, encourage growth and investment, and align with the objectives of the SBSR Act. 

**4.7 Name of Insurance Companies:** The SBSR Act has introduced stipulations with regard to the name of the insurer i.e. the name of the insurer shall contain ‘insurance’, ‘assurance’ or ‘reinsurance’ word in it. In view of the same, it is proposed to introduce 

a new Regulation 56A relating to name of insurers. It also provides the transitional timeline for existing insurers and prior approval requirement for change in name of insurers.

chunks.heading: 5. Ease of Doing Business Measures:
chunks.attachment: परामर्श दस्तावेज़-आईआरडीएआई(बीमाकर्ताओं का पंजीकरण,पूँजी विन्यास,शेयरों का अंतरण और समामेलन) (प्रथम संशोधन),विनियम,2026_Consultation Paper-IRDAI(Registration,Capital Structure,Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers)(1st Amendment)Regulations,.pdf
chunks.content:
The proposed amendments incorporate several measures aimed at improving ease of doing business, including: 

- Reduction in processing fees for amalgamation applications and processing fees for transfer of shares applications. 

- Removal of valuation certification requirements at the registration stage. 

- Providing clarity with regard to approval requirements for issuance of shares at premium, and 

- Formalisation of registration application procedures. 

These measures are expected to reduce operational burden, facilitate investment, support restructuring transactions and enhance efficiency within the insurance sector.

chunks.heading: 6. Protection of Policyholder Interests and Regulatory Safeguards:
chunks.attachment: परामर्श दस्तावेज़-आईआरडीएआई(बीमाकर्ताओं का पंजीकरण,पूँजी विन्यास,शेयरों का अंतरण और समामेलन) (प्रथम संशोधन),विनियम,2026_Consultation Paper-IRDAI(Registration,Capital Structure,Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers)(1st Amendment)Regulations,.pdf
chunks.content:
While facilitating ease of doing business and enhanced investment, the proposed amendments continue to prioritise protection of policyholder interests. The proposed safeguards include: 

- Restricting permissible foreign promoters to (i) incorporated entities (ii) from FATF compliant jurisdictions. 

- Introducing safeguards for amalgamation transactions involving non-insurance companies including restriction on utilisation of policyholder’s fund for the payment of consideration.

chunks.heading: **1. Executive Summary**
chunks.attachment: attachment-2
chunks.content:
The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) proposes amendments to the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (Registration, Capital Structure, Transfer of Shares and Amalgamation of Insurers) Regulations, 2024 (“Registration Regulations”), in light of the changes introduced through the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025 (“SBSR Act”). 

The proposed amendments seek to align the existing regulatory framework with the revised provisions of the Insurance Act, 1938 and facilitate a transparent, growthoriented, and globally aligned insurance ecosystem while ensuring continued protection of policyholder interests. 

The proposed changes relate to regulatory framework regarding: 

- Eligibility criteria for Indian Promoters and Foreign Promoters, 

- Foreign investment safeguards, 

- Special purpose vehicles (SPVs), 

- Approval for Transfer of shares, 

- Amalgamation of insurance companies with non-insurance companies, 

- Processing fees for applications, 

- Name of insurance companies, 

- Forms and application procedures, and 

- Other consequential and clarificatory amendments. 

The amendments are also intended to support: 

- Ease of doing business, 

- Simplification of regulatory processes, 

- Reduction of compliance costs, 

- Enhancement of operational clarity, 

- Facilitation of capital infusion. 

The Authority invites comments from insurers, promoters, investors, professional bodies, legal experts, policyholders, and all other stakeholders on the proposed amendments.

chunks.heading: **2. Existing Regulatory Framework and Legislative Changes under the SBSR Act, 2025**
chunks.attachment: attachment-2

chunks.heading: **3. Objectives of the Proposed Amendments:** The proposed amendments aim to:
chunks.attachment: attachment-2

chunks.heading: 4. Proposed Amendments to Registration Regulations:
chunks.attachment: attachment-2
chunks.content:
Key Amendments are as under: 

**4.1 Definitions and Eligibility Criteria:** Amendments are proposed in definitions relating to ‘Foreign Promoter’, ‘Indian Promoter’ and ‘Special Purpose Vehicle’. The proposed amendments seek to ensure consistency, remove interpretational ambiguities, strengthen regulatory oversight and put in place safeguard mechanisms in view of increase in limit of FDI.

chunks.heading: **4.2 Registration and Application Framework:** The proposed amendments include:
chunks.attachment: attachment-2
chunks.content:
- (a) Insertion of Forms IRDAI/R1 and IRDAI/R2 into the Regulations, 

- (b) Inclusion of Schedule references for Form IRDAI/R3, 

- (c) Introduction of processing fees for No Objection Certificate (NOC) applications; and 

- (d) Restructuring of provisions relating to insurer names. 

These amendments seek to formalise application procedures, improve regulatory clarity and ensure consistency with statutory requirements. 

**4.3 Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs):** The existing framework permits SPVs to act as promoter. The proposed amendment requires applicants to demonstrate the necessity of SPV structures while extending SPV recognition to eligible foreign incorporated entities from Financial Action Task Force (FATF) compliant jurisdictions. 

**4.4 Transfer of Shares and Shareholding Approvals:** The proposed amendments include: 

- (a) Clarification regarding applicability of approval in case of renunciation in rights issues, 

- (b) Alignment with revised Section 6A thresholds. 

- (c) Clarification that approval thresholds apply for every multiple of 5%, and 

- (d) Rationalisation of processing fees. 

These amendments seek to align the Regulations with amended statutory provisions, reduce ambiguities and simplify compliance obligations. 

**4.5 Amalgamation Framework:** The SBSR Act enables amalgamation of insurers with non-insurance companies. Accordingly, a regulatory framework is proposed covering eligible non-insurance entities, conditions for amalgamation, manner of payment of consideration, post-amalgamation safeguards, and measures for protection of policyholder interests. 

**4.6 Rationalisation of Fees Payable by Insurers:** The Authority has been emphasising on rationalising the expenses of management by the insurers to increase affordability of insurance premium at the end of policyholders and also to ensure growth and development of insurance companies as well.

chunks.heading: **4.2 Registration and Application Framework:** The proposed amendments include:
chunks.attachment: attachment-2
chunks.content:
**4.6 Rationalisation of Fees Payable by Insurers:** The Authority has been emphasising on rationalising the expenses of management by the insurers to increase affordability of insurance premium at the end of policyholders and also to ensure growth and development of insurance companies as well. To facilitate the same, it is proposed to rationalise various fees payable by insurers, as under: 

- **Processing fee for Amalgamation Applications:** Current regulations requires payment of fee by each of the insurer at the rate of 1/10<sup>th</sup> of Gross Premium, subject to minimum of Rs.50 lakh and maximum of Rs.5 crores, payable separately by each insurer. It is proposed to reduce the said fees to a fixed amount of Rs.10 lakh, payable by each of the transacting parties. 

- **Processing fee for Transfer of Shares Application:** In case of transfer of more than 50% equity, the existing regulations stipulates application fee at Rs.50 lakh. The same is proposed to be reduced to Rs.10 lakh. 

The proposed reduction in fees is intended to support ease of doing business, reduce compliance costs, encourage growth and investment, and align with the objectives of the SBSR Act. 

**4.7 Name of Insurance Companies:** The SBSR Act has introduced stipulations with regard to the name of the insurer i.e. the name of the insurer shall contain ‘insurance’, ‘assurance’ or ‘reinsurance’ word in it. In view of the same, it is proposed to introduce 

a new Regulation 56A relating to name of insurers. It also provides the transitional timeline for existing insurers and prior approval requirement for change in name of insurers.

chunks.heading: 5. Ease of Doing Business Measures:
chunks.attachment: attachment-2
chunks.content:
The proposed amendments incorporate several measures aimed at improving ease of doing business, including: 

- Reduction in processing fees for amalgamation applications and processing fees for transfer of shares applications. 

- Removal of valuation certification requirements at the registration stage. 

- Providing clarity with regard to approval requirements for issuance of shares at premium, and 

- Formalisation of registration application procedures. 

These measures are expected to reduce operational burden, facilitate investment, support restructuring transactions and enhance efficiency within the insurance sector.

chunks.heading: 6. Protection of Policyholder Interests and Regulatory Safeguards:
chunks.attachment: attachment-2
chunks.content:
While facilitating ease of doing business and enhanced investment, the proposed amendments continue to prioritise protection of policyholder interests. The proposed safeguards include: 

- Restricting permissible foreign promoters to (i) incorporated entities (ii) from FATF compliant jurisdictions. 

- Introducing safeguards for amalgamation transactions involving non-insurance companies including restriction on utilisation of policyholder’s fund for the payment of consideration.

chunks.content:
The Authority invites comments from stakeholders on the proposed amendments. Exposure draft of the Regulation is as per Annexure 1. Stakeholders may submit their comments on the Exposure Draft in the format specified in Annexure 2. Feedback may be submitted to Mr. Nirmal Jain at <u>nirmal.jain@irdai.gov.in</u> with a copy to Mr. Mahesh Agarwal at <u>maheshagarwal@irdai.gov.in on or before 6</u><sup>th</sup> July, 2026. 

**This Consultation Paper is issued for public and industry comments.**
